stimulus.content: Coral reefs are found in warm, shallow ocean waters. They are home to thousands of species of fish, plants, and other sea creatures. Coral reefs are sometimes called the rainforests of the sea because of the huge variety of life they support.

stem: Why are coral reefs compared to rainforests in the passage?

choices.text: Both support a huge variety of living things
Both are found on dry land near the equator
Both have very few species living in them
Both are located in cold deep ocean waters

answer_explanation: Coral reefs are compared to rainforests because both ecosystems support an enormous variety of species. Coral reefs are among the most biodiverse ecosystems in the ocean.
